lessons_one_line: sweep evidence break_reasons[].reason carries file:line directly; no need to rerun sweep with TORCH_LOGS when explain_results.json is available
parametrize.weight_norm generates a fresh ParametrizedConv1d subclass per instance; loop over fresh holders triggers ___check_type_id churn cleanly

BUILD_STRING SymNodeVariable break needs a prior data-dependent break (e.g. inputs_embeds[mask]) to force a resumption frame; without that, FORMAT_VALUE wraps SymInt as LazySymNodeFormatString and BUILD_STRING is happy.

cited line 840 (np.random.rand().item()) is dynamo-traced numpy creating unbacked SymFloat; downstream int() math at line 844/848 is the actual data-dependent break — sweep msg says aten._local_scalar_dense, current nightly raises UserError data-dependent-expression instead, same root cause; tiny config (1 layer, hidden=16) reproduces in 12.5s.
nn.Linear constructed inside forward triggers nn.Parameter ctor break; provenance anchor was literally the cited line (Linear.__init__ self.weight = Parameter)
Direct lift of `with threading.Lock():` from cited line worked first try; ~15-line MRE reproduces with backend=eager fullgraph=True.
logging.Logger method break is generic & model-agnostic; ~13-line MRE with logger.warning() inside @torch.compile fullgraph=True triggers gb0291 immediately — no transformers, no model deps.

Mutation-class GB (setattr on Tensor.requires_grad): provenance was direct (file:line in break_reason); ~22-line MRE with no transformers dep, no dynamic=True needed, hard-error so fullgraph=True + backend=eager.
Python-stdlib primitive (ContextVar.get) sub-pattern: lift literal stdlib call into trivial nn.Module.forward; same file as #11 lock-CM break, ~22 lines, no dynamic=True needed.
sweep gb0170 (Data-dependent branching) and current nightly gb0213 (builtin lt on tensor args) are the same root cause; lifted random.uniform()+self.layerdrop branch into 30-line nn.Module

Torch-internal anchor sub-pattern: hooks.py:27 (RemovableHandle.next_id += 1) is gb0059 setattr-on-class-type — trigger is calling register_*_hook from inside compiled forward; lifts to ~21 lines with no transformers dep; first-shot reproduction.

function.__get__ binding bug: Dynamo classifies the function as SkipFunctionVariable; lift the literal _func.__get__(obj) call into a plain nn.Module forward (~22 lines, no transformers dep, no dynamic=True needed).

Issue title (RNG seeding) was wrong; sweep evidence at line 364 was requires_grad_() intermediate leaked as output (gb8985 -> nightly gb7324 symptom drift). MRE = literal cited line wrapped in trivial fn.

Title misleading: not try/except — `is_torchdynamo_compiling()` guard skips auto-mask-creation, leaving attention_mask=None which TypeErrors on subscript at line 687; ~22-line MRE captures the gated-construction-then-unconditional-use pattern.
gb0208 (torch.* op returned non-Tensor) on np.array.item(): the .item() returning Python int triggers without dynamic=True — Tensor.item gets wrapped even on numpy-array path; minimal MRE 12 lines.
Generic data-dep branching gap (torch.all(mask==1) fast-path); model-agnostic — lifted branch into trivial nn.Module, no transformers dep needed.

Strategy A — sweep fragment 'DictIterator()'/'DictItemsIterator()' Reconstruction failure (gb0092) does not surface on current nightly 2.12.0.dev20260407 (sweep was 2.13.0a0+gitf8d66d2); Dynamo absorbs the upstream ContextVar.set break (gb0156) before the in-flight iterator reconstruction codegen runs. MRE faithfully exercises cited lines 224/239 dict-comp pattern; verify_repro signal uses current-nightly fragment 'Encountered graph break that we cannot resume from' per symptom-string drift sub-pattern.

gb0007 Tensor.view ("cannot determine source file" / unknown module) reproduces directly with one SymInt size arg from torch.div(rounding_mode="trunc") under dynamic=True; ~22-line MRE with no transformers dep; sweep + current-nightly fragment match exactly.

Title misleading (says "Gemma 4" + "2 models" + "36 breaks"; sweep evidence shows Gemma3ForConditionalGeneration only, 6 breaks/mode, dominant op aten.nonzero from boolean-mask indexing inputs_embeds[special_image_mask].numel(); symptom-string drift across nightly versions — sweep showed hard graph break at aten.nonzero, current nightly traces it via unbacked SymInt and surfaces as "Runtime assertion failed for expression Eq(u0, ...)"; ~20-line MRE no transformers dep.

Strategy A baseline cut: lift the cited if-statement and the torch.max source op into a 30-line nn.Module; tgt_sizes=2x2 long tensor reproduces on first try; multi-gap title ok to repro the first/canonical gap (data-dependent branch).

task suggested reversed(ModuleList) at models.py:395 but that gap is fixed in current nightly (probe with explain on real model returned 0 graph breaks for that line); pivoted to still-firing Tensor.item() at commons.py:104 (jit.script + tensor-indexed slicing); warn-only class needs TORCH_LOGS=graph_breaks + fullgraph=False, NOT fullgraph=True (which silently suppressed the symptom)
